Your guide to the
best of Ottawa,
every Thursday.

A weekly digest of the city's best events, food finds, and outdoor adventures — curated by someone who actually lives here. No politics, no crime, just Ottawa at its best.

Join 1,200+ Ottawa readers getting this every Thursday.

Curated every week

Not an algorithm. A real person picks what goes in — things worth your time.

Only the good stuff

Arts, food, outdoors, and community. No crime, no politics, no negativity.

Latest Issue

Spring Sounds & Cherry Blossoms

March 18, 2026 · Issue #47

Read full issue →

Editor's Note

"Spring came in fast this year. One day it was -12 and grey, the next the cherry trees along the Canal were doing their thing. This is the Ottawa I love — no preamble, just suddenly beautiful. Enjoy this one."
Featured This Week

Ottawa Jazz Festival – Spring Preview Night

Saturday, March 21 · 7:30 PMNAC Southam Hall, 1 Elgin St

The city's favourite jazz celebration kicks off its spring edition with an intimate preview night. Local legend Colleen Allen leads a quartet through standards and originals, joined by a Montreal bassist making his Ottawa debut. Drinks in the lobby from 6:30.

Get Tickets & Details

Dow's Lake Cherry Blossom Walk

All weekend · Dawn to duskDow's Lake Pavilion, 1001 Queen Elizabeth Dr

Peak bloom is here. The ornamental cherry trees along the Canal are absolutely putting on a show this year — experts are calling it the best season in a decade. Go early to beat the crowds. Free, always.

More info

Spring Craft Beer Festival

Friday–Sunday · Various timesLansdowne Park, Aberdeen Pavilion

Over 40 Ontario craft breweries pour their spring seasonals. Try the wild ales, fruited sours, and the smoked maple porter from Beau's that's getting serious attention this year.

More info

There's more in this issue.

Past Issues

From the Archive

Browse all issues →

Mar 18, 2026

Spring Sounds & Cherry Blossoms

Jazz returns to the Canal, cherry blossoms hit peak bloom at Dow's Lake, and the Carp Farmers' Market reopens for the season.

Mar 11, 2026

St. Patrick's Weekend & Maple Season

Ottawa's St. Pat's parade is back on Sparks Street, maple syrup season hits its peak in the Valley, and a new ramen spot opens in Centretown.

Mar 4, 2026

Winter's Last Dance

The Rideau Canal Skateway closes for the season, Winterlude's final weekend delivers, and a hidden Hintonburg gallery opens a stunning textile show.

Feb 25, 2026

Reading Week & Cozy Spots

Winterlude reaches its midpoint, a new Byward Market bakery is causing lineups, and the Ottawa Philharmonic closes out its February run.
